title: RenderEnvironments Object
description: The list of available render environments. This represents the list of environments shown in the "Scene Settings" dialog as being in the "Fusion Library". It does not include a custom environment, if one has been loaded.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: item
methods.description: Method that returns the specified render environment using an index into the collection.

methods.name: itemById
methods.description: Returns the render environment with the specified ID.

methods.name: itemByName
methods.description: Returns the specified render environment using the name as seen in the user interface.

properties.name: count
properties.description: The number of render environments in the collection.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

classType.title: RenderEnvironments.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

item.title: RenderEnvironments.item Method
item.description: Method that returns the specified render environment using an index into the collection.
item.return_description: Returns the specified render environment or null if an invalid index was specified.

parameters.name: index
parameters.description: The index of the item within the collection. The first item has an index of 0.

itemById.title: RenderEnvironments.itemById Method
itemById.description: Returns the render environment with the specified ID.
itemById.return_description: Returns the specified render environment or null if the ID does not match a render environment.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the render environment to return.

itemByName.title: RenderEnvironments.itemByName Method
itemByName.description: Returns the specified render environment using the name as seen in the user interface.
itemByName.return_description: Returns the specified render environment or null if there's no match on the name.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The name of the render environment to return.

count.title: RenderEnvironments.count Property
count.description: The number of render environments in the collection.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = renderEnvironments_var.count

isValid.title: RenderEnvironments.isValid Property
isValid.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = renderEnvironments_var.isValid

objectType.title: RenderEnvironments.objectType Property
objectType.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object. It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = renderEnvironments_var.objectType
